# Multiplayer Form with Per-Field Typing Indicators and Field-Lock Overlay

## Background

Liveblocks is a realtime collaboration infrastructure that exposes Presence (ephemeral per-user state) and Storage (CRDT-backed persistent state) through React hooks like `useMyPresence`, `useOthers`, `useStorage`, and `useMutation`. Your project is a Next.js (App Router) app that already renders a static form skeleton (Name / Email / Bio fields) but has no realtime behavior wired up. Your job is to turn it into a multiplayer form: every keystroke is synced across browsers via Liveblocks Storage, and every focused/typing user is visible to others via Liveblocks Presence, with a per-field "X is typing in Bio…" indicator and an optimistic per-field lock overlay.

You MUST use the real Liveblocks cloud — DO NOT mock the Liveblocks SDK, DO NOT stub the WebSocket, and DO NOT swap in a fake provider. The runtime environment will provide a real `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` (server-side) and `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` (client-side, public key starting with `pk_`).

## Requirements

* Implement a Liveblocks-backed multiplayer form using `@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` (App Router compatible).
* Storage: a single `LiveObject` named `form` holding three string fields: `name`, `email`, `bio`. Initialize them to empty strings if the room has no storage yet.
* Presence: each user has a `typing` field whose value is one of `"name"`, `"email"`, `"bio"`, or `null`, plus an `info` (or `user`) field with a stable display name (e.g. derived from query-string `?name=Alice`, falling back to `Anonymous`).
* Per-field input: each of the three `<input>`/`<textarea>` elements must be controlled by the value from Storage; every keystroke must write back into the `LiveObject` (e.g. with `useMutation`). The same field MUST stay in sync across all open browser tabs/contexts connected to the same room.
* Per-field typing indicator: above each field, when at least one *other* user has `presence.typing` equal to that field id, render a visible indicator line whose text contains both the user's display name and the human-readable field label (Name / Email / Bio). Examples that are acceptable: `Alice is typing in Bio...`, `Alice is typing in Bio…`. The indicator MUST disappear when that other user blurs / stops typing in the field.
* Optimistic per-field lock overlay: when at least one *other* user is currently `typing` in a field, the corresponding input for the local user MUST show a visually disabled overlay (greyed out / semi-transparent). The input itself MUST remain functionally usable — the local user can still focus it and take over typing (this is a soft lock, not a hard `disabled`). The overlay element MUST be detectable from the DOM (see Acceptance Criteria for the required marker).
* The room id must be the same for every visitor of the page so two browser contexts collaborate in the same room. Hardcode the room id as `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` (read `ZEALT_RUN_ID` from the environment at build/start time; if unset, fall back to `harbor-typing-form-local`). This isolates each evaluation run on the real Liveblocks cloud.
* The Liveblocks auth endpoint MUST be implemented on the server (e.g. `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts`) using `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY`. The client MUST authenticate via this endpoint (i.e. `LiveblocksProvider` with `authEndpoint: "/api/liveblocks-auth"`). Do NOT ship the secret key to the browser.

## Implementation Hints

* The starter project is at `/home/user/myproject`. Inspect the existing form markup in `app/page.tsx` (or equivalent) and replace its static state with Liveblocks-driven state.
* `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` are already installed; don't reinstall.
* Wire `LiveblocksProvider` + `RoomProvider` around the form. Use `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", bio: "" }) }}` and `initialPresence={{ typing: null, info: { name: <displayName> } }}` (field names are flexible as long as Presence/Storage shapes meet the requirements).
* Use `useStorage((root) => root.form)` to read, and `useMutation(({ storage }, field, value) => storage.get("form").set(field, value), [])` to write.
* Use `useUpdateMyPresence()` on `onFocus`/`onBlur`/`onChange` to keep `presence.typing` synced with the field the local user is editing.
* Use `useOthers()` to compute, per field, the list of OTHER users currently typing in that field; render the indicator and the lock overlay based on that list.
* For the overlay, an absolutely-positioned `<div data-testid="lock-overlay-<field>">` on top of the input works well. Pointer-events must NOT permanently disable the input — keep the underlying input reachable by keyboard / click so a user can take over.
* Read the display name from `searchParams.get("name")` on the client; fall back to `"Anonymous"`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: npm run start (a production build will already exist at verification time; the verifier will rebuild and start as needed)
* Port: 3000
* Route: `GET /` renders a form page with at least three labeled inputs/textarea elements for `Name`, `Email`, and `Bio`.
  * The `Name` input has `data-testid="input-name"` and is a single-line `<input>`.
  * The `Email` input has `data-testid="input-email"` and is a single-line `<input>`.
  * The `Bio` input has `data-testid="input-bio"` and is a `<textarea>` or multi-line input.
* Per-field typing indicator marker: when an OTHER user is typing in a field, an element with `data-testid="typing-indicator-<field>"` is rendered above that field (where `<field>` is one of `name`, `email`, `bio`). Its visible text MUST include both that user's display name and the field's human-readable label (`Name`, `Email`, or `Bio`).
* Per-field lock overlay marker: when an OTHER user is typing in a field, an element with `data-testid="lock-overlay-<field>"` is rendered over that field. When NO other user is typing in that field, this overlay element is NOT in the DOM.
* Storage sync: typing in any field in browser context A is reflected in the value of the corresponding input in browser context B within \~2 seconds, when both contexts are connected to the same room.
* Presence cleanup: when the typing user blurs the field, the indicator and the lock overlay in other contexts MUST disappear within \~2 seconds.
* The Liveblocks auth endpoint is reachable at `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` and returns `200` (or `401` only when the request is unauthenticated in a way the implementer chose) for a normal client request initiated by `LiveblocksProvider`.
* The room id MUST be derived from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable as `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` so concurrent verification runs do not collide.
* The Liveblocks SDK packages (`@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`) MUST be used. No mock provider or fake WebSocket may be substituted.

input.result:
I have implemented the multiplayer form with per-field typing indicators and soft-lock overlays using Liveblocks.

Key changes:
1.  **Liveblocks Configuration**: Created `liveblocks.config.ts` to set up the Liveblocks client and room context with typed Presence and Storage.
2.  **Auth Endpoint**: Implemented a Next.js App Router API route at `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts` that uses `@liveblocks/node` to authorize users and assign them a display name (from the `name` query parameter or "Anonymous").
3.  **Room Wrapper**: Created a `Room` component in `app/Room.tsx` that provides the `LiveblocksProvider` and `RoomProvider`, initializing storage with a `LiveObject` containing `name`, `email`, and `bio`.
4.  **Multiplayer Form**: Updated `app/page.tsx` to:
    *   Use `useStorage` to sync form values in real-time.
    *   Use `useMutation` for optimistic storage updates on every keystroke.
    *   Use `useUpdateMyPresence` to track which field the local user is typing in.
    *   Use `useOthers` to display typing indicators (`data-testid="typing-indicator-<field>"`) and lock overlays (`data-testid="lock-overlay-<field>"`) when other users are editing a field.
    *   Ensure the soft lock overlay is visually present but allows the local user to take over the field if needed.
5.  **Room Isolation**: The room ID is dynamically generated as `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` (falling back to `local`) to ensure isolation between different evaluation runs.

The form is now fully collaborative and meets all the specified requirements.
